title: What fMRI Actually Measures
script_summary: fMRI tracks blood-oxygen changes, not thoughts, revealing the complexity of brain imaging.

segments.voiceover: fMRI doesn't watch thoughts — it tracks blood-oxygen changes linked to brain activity.
This is called the BOLD signal. But what does BOLD really mean?
When neurons fire, they need more oxygen... and that’s where the blood comes in.
Researchers measure these changes, but interpreting them isn’t straightforward.
That’s why reverse inference can be misleading; it’s not always what it seems.
Imagine thinking you know what someone is feeling just by their scan... but it’s far more complex.
Want to dive deeper into neuroscience? Hit that subscribe button for more!
